channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.117
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.117 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Introducing OpenAI's GPT-image-2 in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Take a small design team running a global social campaign. They have the creative vision to produce localized imagery for every market, but not the resources to reshoot, reformat, or outsource that scale. Every asset needs to fit a different platform, a different dimension, a different cultural...
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny

item.title: Ingest at Scale, Securely — Azure Monitor pipeline Is Now Generally Available
item.description: Today, we're thrilled to announce the general availability of Azure Monitor pipeline — a telemetry pipeline built for secure, high-scale ingestion across any environment. But the best way to understand what makes it powerful isn't to start with features. It's to start with the problems that kept...
item.dc:creator: XemaPathak

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description: Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available.Azure Monitor pipeline provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation, designed specifically for secure, high‑throughput, enterprise‑scale scenarios. It's built on open-sou
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 4
item.description: The Agent-Ready Database: Security, Backup, and MCP Part 4 of 4 – The Multi-Model Database Series This is the final post in a four-part series on multi-model databases in SQL Server 2025 and Azure SQL – exploring how the optimizer, storage engine, and security layer treat each data model as a...
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: .NET 10.0.7 Out-of-Band Security Update
item.description: We are releasing .NET 10.0.7 as an out-of-band security update to address CVE-2026-40372. The post .NET 10.0.7 Out-of-Band Security Update appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT)

item.title: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection
item.description: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection (ANF ARP) is now in GA (General Availability). ANF ARP is designed to help organizations proactively detect, respond to, and recover from ransomware threats on cloud volumes. ANF ARP monitors Azure NetApp
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: I Wasted 68 Minutes a Day Re-Explaining My Code. Then I Built auto-memory.
item.description: ~1,900 lines of Python. Zero dependencies. Saves you an hour a day. GitHub → · pip install auto-memory Now give Copilot CLI enhanced context recall. Point it at deploy/install.md and let it cook. 🍳 Are you tired of using the slash /compact command every 10 min? The Context Window Is a Lie Every AI...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: General Availability: Dynamic Data Masking for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Protecting sensitive data is a foundational requirement for modern applications. As organizations scale their use of Azure Cosmos DB across teams, environments, and workloads, controlling who can see sensitive information becomes increasingly important. Today, we are excited to announce the general...
item.dc:creator: Sudhanshu Khera

item.title: Aspire 13.2: Bun Support and Container Enhancements
item.description: Aspire 13.2 keeps the JavaScript story moving with Bun for Vite, more predictable containers, and small but meaningful fixes across debugging and builds. The post Aspire 13.2: Bun Support and Container Enhancements appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. You can now define custom sanitizers and validators using data extensions… The post CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Detection strategies across cloud and identities against infiltrating IT workers
item.description: The shift to remote and hybrid work since the pandemic expanded global hiring and accelerated digital onboarding, increasing reliance on online identity verification and remote access. The post Detection strategies across cloud and identities against infiltrating IT workers appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Announcing public preview of redundant TCP support for RDP Multipath for Azure Virtual Desktop
item.description: Reliable connectivity is essential for ensuring consistent productivity in Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) environments. Network variability—whether due to packet loss, NAT misconfiguration, UDP‑restricted networks, or restrictive enterprise network policies—continues to be one of the most common...
item.dc:creator: Rinku_Dalwani

item.title: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Building the Thread, Sending the Reply, and Operating in Production (2/2)🔥
item.description: aka, turns out email-safe HTML is basically a different language
item.dc:creator: Alex Moore

item.title: Azure DevOps MCP Server April Update
item.description: This update brings a set of improvements and changes across both local and remote Azure DevOps MCP Servers. Here’s a summary of what’s changed. Query work items with WIQL We’ve introduced a new wit_query_by_wiql tool that enables users to construct and run work item WIQL queries. For our remote...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Getting Started with Agentic DevOps – Part 1: Foundations
item.description: This post is the first in a 3-part series: Foundations (this post) Start shipping (context engineering, MCP servers, orchestration) App modernization (Java, .NET, PHP, microservices) Bookmark this post for quick reference as you start exploring Agentic DevOps. It will be updated as the 3 parts...
item.dc:creator: Ricardo Fiel

item.title: Claim your IQ Series: Foundry IQ badge
item.description: The IQ Series kicked off with three Foundry IQ episodes, each paired with a hands-on cookbook. If you've worked through all three or you're planning to, there's now a digital badge waiting for you to claim! What the badge represents The IQ Series: Foundry IQ badge recognizes developers who've...
item.dc:creator: aycabas

item.title: Leveraging Azure Resource Graph Queries for Azure Redis Configuration
item.description: Scenario Many times, we receive requests for a quick and reliable way to review Azure Redis configurations such as SKU tiers, Redis versions, TLS settings, Microsoft Entra authentication status, and public network exposure. Traditionally, these checks are performed using PowerShell, Azure CLI, or...
item.dc:creator: Soma_Sekhara_Raju

item.title: Leveraging Azure Resource Graph Queries for Azure Storage Configuration
item.description: Scenario Many times, we receive requests for a quick and reliable way to check which Azure Storage features are enabled across subscriptions—such as SFTP, Hierarchical Namespace (HNS), or default access tiers. For such scenarios, customers can use PowerShell, Azure CLI, or REST APIs; however, these...
item.dc:creator: jainsourabh

item.title: How to Troubleshoot Azure Functions Host Startup Issue
item.description: Overview Azure Functions is a powerful serverless compute service that enables you to run event-driven code without managing infrastructure. When you deploy a Function App, the Azure Functions host is the runtime process responsible for discovering your functions, loading extensions and bindings,...
item.dc:creator: vikasgupta5

item.title: From Playwright Automation to Agent Driven Testing (GHCP in Action)
item.description: What Is Agent-Driven Testing? Agent-driven testing represents a revolutionary shift from traditional, hardcoded test automation to intelligent, adaptive testing powered by AI agents. Unlike conventional Playwright tests that rely on static selectors and predefined workflows, agent-driven testing...
item.dc:creator: syedarshad

item.title: Service Bus SBMP Retirement: What BizTalk Server 2020 Customers Need to Know
item.description: On September 30, 2026, the Azure Service Bus team will retire support for the Service Bus Messaging Protocol (SBMP). This is important BizTalk Server 2020 customers who use the BizTalk Service Bus (SB-Messaging) adapter, as SBMP is the protocol that adapter relies on today. To help customers...
item.dc:creator: hcamposu

item.title: GitHub Copilot meets Azure Developer CLI: AI-assisted project setup and error troubleshooting
item.description: The Azure Developer CLI (azd) now integrates with GitHub Copilot for AI-assisted project scaffolding and intelligent deployment error troubleshooting, helping you resolve common Azure errors like MissingSubscriptionRegistration, SkuNotAvailable, and StorageAccountAlreadyTaken without leaving the...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: Writing Node.js addons with .NET Native AOT
item.description: The C# Dev Kit team replaced C++ Node.js addons with C# and Native AOT, removing a dependency on Python. This post walks through building a Node.js native addon entirely in C# using N-API, LibraryImport, and UnmanagedCallersOnly. The post Writing Node.js addons with .NET Native AOT appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Drew Noakes

item.title: v1.14.9
item.description: 1.14.9 (April 20, 2026) BUG FIXES: Fix Terraform Stacks plugin installation error (#38406)
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans
item.description: We're making these changes to ensure a reliable and predictable experience for existing customers. The post Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Joe Binder

item.title: Troubleshoot with OpenTelemetry in Azure Monitor - Public Preview
item.description: OpenTelemetry is fast becoming the industry standard for modern telemetry collection and ingestion pipelines. With Azure Monitor’s new OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) support, you can ship logs, metrics, and traces from wherever you run workloads to analyze and act on your observability data in one...
item.dc:creator: KayodePrince

item.title: Autonomous AKS Incident Response with Azure SRE Agent: From Alert to Verified Recovery in Minutes
item.description: When a Sev1 alert fires on an AKS cluster, detection is rarely the hard part. The hard part is what comes next: proving what broke, why it broke, and fixing it without widening the blast radius, all under time pressure, often at 2 a.m. Azure SRE Agent is designed to close that gap. It connects...
item.dc:creator: hailukassa

item.title: Secure, Keyless Application Access with Managed Identities - Now GA in Azure Files SMB
item.description: As enterprises modernize applications and strengthen their security posture, identity is central to how applications access shared storage. Traditional identity models relying on account keys, stored credentials, or domain‑joined infrastructure add operational overhead and introduce security risks...
item.dc:creator: Priyanka-Gangal

item.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description: As shared in our recent blog post, we’re making the following changes to Copilot plans for individuals as part of our ongoing efforts to ensure service reliability and a sustainable… The post Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Monitor supports native OTLP ingestion using the Azure Monitor Agent
item.description: Azure Monitor now supports native ingestion of OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) signals. Send telemetry directly from OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications by using the Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) to receive OTLP from your apps and export it to Azure Monitor
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Highlights from Git 2.54
item.description: The open source Git project just released Git 2.54. Here is GitHub’s look at some of the most interesting features and changes introduced since last time. The post Highlights from Git 2.54 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Blau

item.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
item.description: How Microsoft secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform by removing credentials, reducing attack surfaces, and using platform engineering to block opportunistic threats. The post Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ilya Grebnov

item.title: Announcing new hybrid deployment options for Azure Virtual Desktop
item.description: Azure Virtual Desktop is a secured, cloud-based virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) service that enables organizations to deliver Windows desktops and applications to users. Originally launched in 2019, Azure Virtual Desktop has evolved rapidly to meet the changing needs of modern workplaces,...
item.dc:creator: Steve_Downs

item.title: Sunsetting SHA-1 in HTTPS on GitHub
item.description: What’s changing We’re going to remove the use of SHA-1 in HTTPS for GitHub and our CDNs. This impacts browsers that are used to view the GitHub website, any software… The post Sunsetting SHA-1 in HTTPS on GitHub appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Troubleshooting Microsoft Foundry Accessing On‑Premises APIs Over Private Networking
item.description: Audience: Azure solution architects, network engineers, and AI practitioners deploying Microsoft Foundry in enterprise, network‑isolated environments. Scenario: Foundry Agent Service must call an on‑premises (or privately hosted) API over VPN or ExpressRoute using on‑premises corporate DNS....
item.dc:creator: pankajag

item.title: Simplify On-prem File share Migration to Azure: Discover & assess suitability using Azure Migrate
item.description: Migrating on‑premises file servers to the cloud is a complex infrastructure transformation—not just a data move. Many organizations lack the visibility needed to decide whether to rehost or modernize file shares spread across Windows and Linux servers. Azure Migrate now extends its discovery and...
item.dc:creator: bapic

item.title: Discoverable – Observable MCP Server
item.description: How to build an observable MCP Server with discoverable tools and agents. The post Discoverable – Observable MCP Server appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Colin Desmond

item.title: Building an Enterprise HR Chatbot with Multi-Strategy RAG and Live Agent Handoff on Azure
item.description: HR teams deal with thousands of employee questions every day — policy lookups, leave balances, case escalations, and sensitive topics like harassment or misconduct. AI chatbots can handle the routine stuff and free up HR advisors for the hard cases. But most chatbot projects get stuck at basic Q&A....
item.dc:creator: risthakur1213

item.title: Network Connectivity Check APIs for Logic App Standard
item.description: Introduction When your Logic App Standard is integrated with a Virtual Network (VNET), you can use these APIs to troubleshoot connectivity issues to downstream resources like SQL databases, Storage Accounts, Service Bus, Key Vault, and more. The checks run directly from the worker hosting your...
item.dc:creator: Mohammed_Barqawi

item.title: AKS App Routing's Next Chapter: Gateway API with Istio
item.description: If you've been following my previous posts on the Ingress NGINX retirement, you'll know the story so far. The community Ingress NGINX project was retired in March 2026, and Microsoft's extended support for the NGINX-based App Routing add-on runs until November 2026. I've covered migrating from...
item.dc:creator: samcogan

item.title: Step by Step Guide to Ontology and Plan for Financial Service
item.description: What We Will Build In this guide, we will construct a complete Fabric IQ solution that accomplishes the following: First, a Lakehouse that ingests publicly available data including bank financials, P2P lending statistics, borrower demographics, and licensing information. Second, a Semantic Model...
item.dc:creator: NaufalPrawironegoro

item.title: Azure Virtual Desktop in the Perth Azure Extended Zone: A Real‑World Production Deployment
item.description: Introduction Perth is one of the most geographically isolated major cities globally. For performance‑sensitive, graphics‑heavy engineering workloads, user experience can degrade significantly when desktops are hosted far from the user. For workloads such as subsurface modelling and GPU‑intensive...
item.dc:creator: akashmishra

item.title: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
item.description: Threat actors are abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration to impersonate IT helpdesk staff and convince users to grant remote access. Once inside, attackers can abuse legitimate tools and standard admin protocols to move laterally and exfiltrate data while appearing as routine IT...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Microsoft Defender Experts

item.title: Microsoft Fabric Operations Agent Step by Step Walkthrough
item.description: Fabric Capacity and Workspace You need a Microsoft Fabric workspace backed by a paid capacity. Trial capacities are not supported for Operations Agent. Your capacity must be provisioned in a supported region. As of April 2026, Operations Agent is available in all Microsoft Fabric regions except...
item.dc:creator: NaufalPrawironegoro

item.title: MCP Demystified: Tools vs Resources vs Prompts Explained Simply
item.description: Introduction When developers start working with Model Context Protocol (MCP), one of the most confusing parts is understanding the difference between MCP Tools, Resources, and Prompts. All three are important components in modern AI application development, but they serve completely different...
item.dc:creator: Ravindra_Kumar_Vishwakarma

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection
item.description: Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in GitHub Copilot CLI for all Copilot plans. With auto, Copilot chooses the most efficient model on your behalf. How it works… The post GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally available: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 on Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 through Azure Databricks AI Model Serving. Claude Opus 4.7 is Anthropic's most capable hybrid reasoning model, delivering stronger performance on complex extraction and agentic reasoning tasks while
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Azure Functions runtime v3 on Linux Consumption will stop running September 30, 2026
item.description: Azure Functions runtime v3 was retired on December 13, 2022. As part of ongoing efforts to reduce reliance on legacy infrastructure and focus investments on supported platforms, Azure will enforce this retirement for Linux Consumption–based Function Apps
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: See how we created an emoji list generator during the Rubber Duck Thursday stream. The post Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Cassidy Williams
